title: Biomedical Engineer
description: ABOUT FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit organization that mobilizes research, resources and relationships so that people everywhere can access the opportunities they need to lead full, healthy lives. Our staff of more than 2,000 experts work in over 50 countries around the world. PROJECT OVERVIEW : The Meeting Targets and Maintaining Epidemic Control (EpiC) project is a global health initiative funded by the U.S. Department of State, running from 2019 to 2027. In the Philippines, EpiC has two workplans each with its own technical scope and budget, one focused on achieving HIV epidemic control and one focused on strengthening global health security (GHS). The role outlined in this job description supports EpiC’s GHS workplan. EpiC’s GHS workplan aims to strengthen national, subnational, and local capacities for the prevention, detection, and response to public health emergencies, including pandemic preparedness. Support is delivered across multiple technical areas, including Zoonotic Diseases, Biosafety and Biosecurity, National Laboratory Systems, Surveillance, Public Health Emergency Management, Infection Prevention and Control (IPC), and Evidence-Based Public Health Messaging. All activities and sub-activities under each technical area are aligned with and informed by national priorities and strategies, including the National Action Plan for Health Security (2025–2030), the National Framework of the Philippine Health Laboratory System (PHLS), and the National Laboratory Strategic Plan (NLSP) 2025–2030. JOB SUMMARY : Provides final authorization and approval of the technical portions of proposals and project design, based on the design and direction set by technical leadership. Drafts the technical portions of proposals and project design, based on the design and direction set by technical leadership. Provides final authorization and approval of the technical portions of proposals and project design, based on the design and direction set by technical leadership. Assists with developing the technical portions of proposals and project design. Provides higher-level administrative support on core project operations (e.g., update manuals, assist with proposals, and budget tracking). Provides technical support services to assigned projects under supervision. Assists with project implementation and training. Contributes to new business development initiatives including project design, budgeting, and recruitment efforts. The Biomedical Engineer will provide specialized technical leadership to strengthen the safety, functionality, and resilience of health facility engineering systems that are critical for outbreak preparedness and emergency response. The position will support the implementation of EpiC/GHS laboratory, biosafety and biosecurity, infection prevention and control (IPC), and emergency readiness by ensuring that diagnostic, life-support, and biosafety equipment are properly assessed, installed, maintained, and operational during routine operations and surge conditions. Working closely with laboratory and diagnostics, IPC, and health emergency management teams the Biomedical Engineer will support priority hospitals, laboratories, and strategic-depth facilities to ensure that engineering systems including cold chain, biosafety cabinets, autoclaves, ventilation, power backup, and waste management—are functional, safe, and compliant with national biosafety and quality standards. The role is essential for translating equipment investments into reliable service delivery, particularly in high-risk regions and emergency contexts. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES : 1. Facility Engineering Readiness for Outbreaks and Emergencies Conduct engineering readiness assessments of priority hospitals, laboratories, and strategic-depth facilities to identify gaps affecting outbreak response and surge capacity. Support implementation of corrective actions related to power supply, ventilation, cold chain, sterilization, and waste handling, that directly affect biosafety, IPC, and diagnostic continuity. Support facility-level contingency planning for engineering failures during outbreaks, disasters, or supply disruptions. 2. Laboratory and Diagnostic Equipment Support Provide technical oversight for installation, calibration, and maintenance of laboratory equipment, including PCR platforms, biosafety cabinets, autoclaves, centrifuges, refrigerators, and portable diagnostic systems. Support laboratory biosafety and biosecurity compliance by ensuring that engineering controls and equipment function according to national and international standards. Work with laboratory teams to document preventive maintenance plans and equipment readiness checklists for outbreak periods. 3. Infection Prevention and Control (IPC) Engineering Controls Support IPC-related engineering interventions, including ventilation optimization, isolation area zoning, donning/doffing areas, and environmental decontamination systems. Assist facilities in improving waste management, sterilization, and sanitation infrastructure to reduce healthcare-associated infection risks during outbreaks. Support integration of engineering IPC indicators into facility emergency readiness assessments. 4. Coordination, Documentation, and Technical Reporting Coordinate closely with laboratory, IPC, and health emergency management teams to ensure engineering interventions are synchronized with operational needs. Prepare technical reports, assessment summaries, and engineering readiness documentation for project monitoring and donor reporting. Provide technical input to SOPs, job aids, and facility guidance related to equipment use, maintenance, and safety. 5. Capacity Strengthening and Knowledge Transfer Mentor biomedical staff and maintenance teams through hands-on coaching and field-based support rather than classroom-only training. Support development of practical job aids and checklists for routine and emergency equipment operation. Participate in simulation exercises, drills, and after-action reviews to ensure engineering systems perform under real-world stress conditions. MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS : Moderate knowledge of concepts, practices, and procedures by providing technical support for medical and laboratory equipment management. Knowledge of medical device safety standards and regulations Has sensitivity to cultural diversity and understanding of the political, contextual, and ethical issues in assigned areas. Articulate, professional and able to communicate in a clear, positive manner with clients and staff. Must be able to read, write, and speak fluent English. Excellent organizational and analytical skills. Demonstrated proficiency with using Microsoft Office Suite required. Ability to analyze and interpret data, identify errors, and prepare reports. Ability to solve problems and implement corrective action as needed. WORKING CONDITIONS AND TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS: Usual office working conditions Ability to travel domestically at least 25% EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent university degree required in mechanical engineering, chemical engineering, biomedical engineering, or related field Preferred licensed engineer or technician with certifications in facilities-related specialties Approximately 5 to 7 years of work experience in facilities and medical equipment management, including maintenance of biomedical, laboratory equipment and related commodities. . Experience conducting hospital-level and/or regional/national training preferred. An in-depth understanding of the context and capabilities of the local health sector is preferred. Substantial previous experience in working with government, private sector, and partners. Experience in COVID-19 response projects or any health emergency response desired. Prior work experience in a non-governmental organization (NGO), government agency, or private company. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. It neither prescribes nor restricts the exact tasks that may be assigned to carry out these duties. This document should not be construed in any way to represent a contract of employment. title: Senior Technical Officer, Global Health Security (GHS) Surveillance
description: The Meeting Targets and Maintaining Epidemic Control (EpiC) is a global project focused on HIV and global health security with funding from the United States Government. EpiC is seeking a qualified and motivated Vietnamese candidate to serve in the role of Senior Technical Officer, Global Health Security (GHS) Surveillance (02 positions) based in Hanoi/Ho Chi Minh City (HCMC) . Main responsibilities: The Senior Technical Officer, GHS Surveillance supports implementation of Global Health Security surveillance activities, ensuring technical quality and adherence to organizational standards. Working under the Technica Advisor, GHS Surveillance, the role contributes to work planning, monitoring and evaluation, and reporting, and supports activity delivery at both central and site levels. The position also responds to technical requests from project leadership. Assist in providing technical support for GHS surveillance activities, focusing on zoonotic and emerging infectious diseases (ZD/EID) under the guidance of the technical advisor. Facilitate the rollout and oversight of integrated and event-based surveillance (IBS/EBS) activities through coordination with government counterparts and partners, as directed. Contribute to the implementation of surveillance activities at Points of Entry (PoE), including data collection, assisting with reporting workflows, and facilitating communication between surveillance and response teams. Monitor day-to-day progress of surveillance activities, keeping records of work plan milestones and deliverables, and reporting progress to the technical advisor. Assist in workplan development by preparing technical inputs, drafting sections of work plans, and supporting budget preparation, as directed. Help ensure surveillance activities, tools, and outputs comply with donor requirements, national guidelines, and organizational SOPs. Implement and monitor the updating, utilization, and maintenance of health information systems (HIS) for surveillance, including data collection tools, reporting workflows, and data quality assurance processes. Prepare technical reports, briefs, presentations, and training materials for internal and external audiences as directed by the technical advisor. Analyze surveillance data to identify gaps and lessons learned, and report findings or issues to the technical advisor or senior technical staff. Coordinate with project teams, technical units, and partners to promote collaboration and information sharing. Other tasks as assigned Experience and abilities for the position: Bachelor’s degree in public health, epidemiology, preventive medicine, or equivalent minimum of five years’ experience in supporting public health surveillance or health intervention programs. Working knowledge of ZD/EID surveillance, including familiarity with IBS, EBS, multisectoral surveillance approaches, POE, and/or health information system. Experience working with international NGOs, the Government of Vietnam (GVN), and donor agencies on to improve surveillance or health outcomes. Ability to contribute to technical writing, including reports, workplan sections, presentations, or briefs. Experience supporting or working with health information systems for surveillance, including data collection, validation, or reporting processes. Strong organizational and analytical skills, with the ability to track deliverables, support work plans, and manage multiple tasks. Ability to work collaboratively in multidisciplinary teams and follow technical direction from senior advisors. Ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, and achieve ambitious results. Well-developed written and oral communication skills in both Vietnamese and English. Proficiency in MS office package (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, etc.), database and spreadsheets with advance skills in at least two of the following programs: SPSS, Excel, Epi Info, Stata, SQL, and Power BI. Ability to travel as necessary. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. It neither prescribes nor restricts the exact tasks that may be assigned to carry out these duties. title: Senior M&E Officer
description: About FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit human development organization dedicated to improving lives in lasting ways by advancing integrated, locally driven solutions. Our staff includes experts in health, education, nutrition, environment, economic development, civil society, gender, youth, research and technology — creating a unique mix of capabilities to address todays interrelated development challenges. FHI 360 serves more than 70 countries and all U.S. states and territories. We are currently seeking qualified candidates for the position of: Senior M&E Officer Description: FHI 360 is seeking a candidate for the position of Senior M&E Officer for EpiC MNCH, a project to be implemented in the provinces of Nampula/Zambezia, focused on improving the quality of maternal, neonatal, and child health outcomes and working with government partners to institutionalize a culture of quality improvement. T he Senior M&E Officer will support with the implementation and strategic coordination of monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) activities for the MNCH & Nutrition component of the EpiC Mozambique project . S /he will provide technical oversight and support to MEL implementation in collaboration with EpiC project staff, provincial level SPS and DPS teams and implementing partners in Zambezia, Nampula and other supported provinces; will ensure that data systems effectively capture, analyze, and report performance results that align with national and project indicators. This position will help strengthen partner MEL systems and performance reporting for community and facility based MNCH and nutrition services, including working with sub-partners and key stakeholders to monitor, document, and analyze program performance, ensure data quality, and support continuous quality improvement (CQI) and adaptive program management across the target provinces. The selected candidate will be based in Zambezia and will report to the Technical Director . Accountab ilities: Support the MOH/N ational D irectorate of P ublic H ealth (DNSP) embedded Nutrition M&E Advisor in the design and implementation of M&E systems and tools for the project. Synthesize reports, analyze data, and develop or adapt M&E tools. Work with the DOS FAO, HQ strategic information teams, project staff, and provincial level health authorities (SPS and DPS) to provide support and guidance on project M&E activities, ensuring alignment with Department of State (DoS) and MOH reporting requirements. Work with implementing partners / local CBOs M &E teams to support the correct implementation and use of M&E tools and platforms (SISNU, and others ), and adherence to complete, correct and timely reporting . Conduct routine monitoring visits to project sites, conduct data quality assessments (DQAs) using official tools, assist in the preparation of weekly and monthly reports, and carry out joint supportive supervision at sites with the partners regional teams and the West Health Directorate . Ensure that the quality of activities adheres to EpiC and DOS standards by supporting the development and implementation of appropriate mechanisms to ensure quality. Provide direct technical assistance and capacity building in the design and implementation of the M&E components of the activities in the communities served. Work closely with the site Data Managers to assist in the development and maintenance of computerized data capture of the program and provide technical assistance and training to the staff at the sites responsible for data entry. Ensure data entry on DATIM and FHI360 Headquarters platforms as needed. Identify and document technical or operational issues encountered in national digital systems such as SIS MA, DHIS , and promptly escalate them to the M&E Advisor and relevant national stakeholders for resolution. Provide technical assistance to project M&E Officers and partner MEL teams and ensure integration of EpiC data into the national HMIS. Build capacity of regional MEL staff in project M&E, especially data collection, management, and analysis for use in decision-making and course correction. Remain informed of current issues regarding M&E of programs in the MNCH and Nutrition field by review of current literature and Haitian protocols, guidelines, and standards. Assist the M&E Advisor in leading coordination of regional MEL activities, ensuring alignment with program and national MEL priorities. Contribute to the preparation of donor reporting deliverables, including narrative reports, performance summaries, and indicator validations. Mentor partner MEL teams and provide strategic guidance on MEL planning, data interpretation, and data visualization. Assist the SI Advisor in facilitating regional data review meetings and data-to-action sessions with implementing partners and key stakeholders. Represent FHI 360 in SI-related meetings at the regional level, including those led by MOH, DOS or other institutional actors. Document lessons learned and best practices in M&E, according to donor guidelines. Ensure documentation of MEL activities and contribute to knowledge-sharing across partners and regional MEL teams. Contribute to the documentation of program results and lessons learned for handover to the MSPP or other partners. Perform other duties as assigned. Required Qualifications: Master’s degree or its international equivalent in public health, statistics, applied economics, epidemiology, social work, monitoring & evaluation, demography, biostatistics, analyses or other relevant disciplines, with 6 years’ relevant work experience. Training or certification in M&E, data analysis, or program evaluation is an asset. Demonstrated MEL experience, at least 6 years, including experience in technical leadership and supervision of MEL teams or partner organizations. Knowledge of health and development programs in Haiti including familiarity with the national health information management system (HMIS) and national protocols, guidelines and standards for MNCH & Nutrition service provision and M& E. Experience with MER indicators and community MNCH service data. Knowledge and experience in national-level M&E system implementation. Demonstrated experience working with DDS and national M&E stakeholders (e.g., PNLS). Previous experience working with USAID or PEPFAR supported programs. Proficiency in data analysis and visualization software such as Power BI. Familiarity with Open Data Kit (ODK), KoboToolbox , or Commcare . Familiarity with DHIS2 environment. Ability to facilitate performance review, data-to-action workshops, and regional learning sessions. Ability to manage tight deadlines and deliver high volumes of work with minimal supervision. Excellent report writing, analytical and communication skills, including oral presentation skills. Fluency in Portuguese and English. Typical Physical Demands : Typical office environment. Ability to spend long hours looking at a computer screen and doing repetitive work on a keyboard. Ability to sit and stand for extended periods of time. Ability to lift/move up to 5 lbs. Technology to be Used : Personal Computer/Laptop, Microsoft applications (i.e., Office 365, SharePoint, Skype/Zoom/Teams), cell phone/mobile technology, and standard office equipment. Travel Requirements : Up to 50% ** Or an equivalent combination of relevant education and/or experience sufficient to successfully perform the key responsibilities of the job. title: Monitoring and Evaluation Officer
description: Job Summary: The Monitoring and Evaluation Officer will lead monitoring, data collection, analysis, and reporting for the GBV training program, ensuring evidence-based implementation and compliance with ABT and DFAT reporting requirements. ACCOUNTABILITIES Monitoring & Data Management Develop and implement the project M&E plan, tools, and indicators aligned with the subcontract. Collect, verify, and manage quantitative and qualitative data from training activities. Ensure data quality, confidentiality, and ethical handling of sensitive GBV-related information. Reporting & Learning Produce regular M&E reports, dashboards, and summaries for internal and donor use. Support outcome tracking, pre/post training assessments, and learning reviews. Document lessons learned, success stories, and recommendations for program improvement. Capacity Support Support training officers and partners on basic data collection and reporting tools. Ensure consistent use of monitoring tools across all provinces. Compliance Ensure alignment with DFAT, ABT, and FHI360 M&E standards. Support audits, evaluations, and performance assessments as required. REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE Bachelor’s degree in monitoring & evaluation, statistics, public health, development studies, or related field. Minimum 3–5 years’ experience in M&E for donor-funded programs. Experience working on GBV, health, or social protection programs is an advantage. Strong analytical, reporting, and data management skills. Familiarity with ethical considerations in GBV data collection PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS Typical office environment; ability to travel domestically and internationally as needed (10-25%). Occasional work outside regular office hours may be required for urgent project needs. TECHNOLOGY TO BE USED Microsoft Office Suita, project management software, and other standard office equipment. title: Training Officer
description: Job Summary: The GBV Training Officer will support the design, delivery, and follow-up of GBV training activities across assigned provinces. The role focuses on building capacity of frontline workers and stakeholders on GBV prevention, response, and referral systems. ACCOUNTABILITIES Training Delivery Deliver GBV training sessions for health workers and relevant stakeholders at provincial and district levels. Support adaptation of training materials to local contexts while maintaining technical integrity. Facilitate participatory, survivor-centered, and trauma-informed learning approaches. Field Coordination Work closely with PHAs and local partners to organize training logistics and participant engagement. Support follow-up mentoring, coaching, and refresher sessions as required. Document field-level challenges, feedback, and good practices. Safeguarding & Ethics Apply survivor-centered principles and confidentiality in all training activities. Uphold FHI360 and DFAT safeguarding, PSEAH, and child protection standards. Report any safeguarding concerns immediately in line with protocols. Reporting Prepare timely activity reports, attendance records, and training evaluations. Contribute to lessons learned and continuous improvement of training approaches. REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE Postgraduate qualification in public health, social sciences, gender studies, development, or related field. Minimum 7–10 years’ experience in GBV programming, training, or gender-focused, community based interventions. Bachelor’s degree in social work, community development, public health, education, or related field. Strong facilitation and adult learning skills. Experience working with provincial health systems or community structures in PNG. Willingness and ability to travel extensively. title: Finance Associates
description: Titre du poste : Finance Associates Lieu d’affectation : (02) Lubumbashi, (01) Mbujimayi, RDC Nombre de postes à couvrir : Trois (3) Type et durée du contrat : Contrat local à durée déterminée (3 mois avec possibilité d’extension) avec une période d’essai de mois. Département/projet : Finances et Operations CONTEXTE : FHI 360 est une organisation internationale de développement qui se distingue par une approche rigoureuse fondée sur des preuves scientifiques. Son personnel comprend des experts en santé, nutrition, éducation, développement économique, société civile, environnement et recherche. FHI 360 opère à partir d’un réseau de 60 bureaux et emploie environ 4,400 personnes aux Etats-Unis et à travers le monde. Son engagement à travailler en partenariat à tous les niveaux et sa compétence pluridisciplinaire lui permettent d’avoir un impact durable sur les individus, les familles, les communautés et les pays auxquels elle apporte son assistance. C’est ainsi que FHI 360 améliore de façon durable le quotidien de millions de personnes. Description du Projet : FHI 360 est actuellement à la recherche des candidats qualifiés pour le poste d’Associé au Finance pour les projets “ Meeting Targets and Maintaining Epidemic Control ” (EpiC) intégré, VIH et Kitumaini en RDC. Epic Intégré est financé par le Département dÉtat des États-Unis (DoS), via le Bureau de la Sécurité Sanitaire Mondiale et de la Diplomatie (GHSD). Le projet vise à accélérer la réduction des décès évitables chez les mères, les nouveaux nés et les enfants en fournissant un paquet dinterventions vitales intégrées couvrant la Santé Maternelle et Infantile (SMNE), la Nutrition, et le Paludisme dans neuf provinces de la République Démocratique du Congo (RDC). Le programme adopte une approche géographique par phases, avec quatre provinces désignées à « Pleine Présence » (Haut Katanga, Lualaba, Kasai Oriental, et Lomami) pour un soutien complet et immédiat, et cinq provinces à « Présence Minimale » pour un soutien ciblé sur le paludisme. Également Epic VIH mise en œuvre principalement dans le grand Katanga est financé par DoS à travers le Plan Présidentiel d’urgence pour la lutte contre le Sida (‘PEPFAR’) . Par ailleurs, le projet Kitumaini, sous le financement du Département d’Etat américain (DoS), apporte son appui technique et financier pour accompagner le Gouvernement dans l’atteinte et le maintien du contrôle de l’épidémie du VIH/SIDA. C’est dans ce cadre que le projet Kitumaini appui la division provinciale de la santé de la province du Haut Katanga dans la lutte contre le VIH/SIDA avec comme l’une des stratégies, intensification de l’index testing au niveau des sites et de la communauté et cela dans zones de santé qu’il appui à savoir Kamalondo, Kampemba, Kenya, Lubumbashi, et Ruashi. DESCRIPTION DU POSTE : Sous la supervision du Finance & Operations Manager de FHI360 , l’Associé au Finance aura pour principales taches de préparer et effectuer les paiements des fournisseurs et partenaires, traiter et enregistre toutes les transactions du projet dans les logiciels comptable DPA et GFAS. Le poste sera basé à Lubumbashi, et Mbujimayi RDC et ouvert uniquement aux ressortissants congolais résidents dans les zones d’affectation Les candidats qualifiés vivant ouvertement avec le VIH et/ou qui sont représentatifs des populations touchées par le VIH sont également encouragés à postuler. RESPONSABILITÉS CLÉS DU POSTE : Recevoir toutes les factures de fournisseurs, vérifier leur exactitude et toute la documentation de support avant leur paiement ; Préparer les paiements des fournisseurs ainsi que ceux des partenaires tout en se rassurant du strict respect des procédures de FHI 360 Enregistre les transactions du projet dans les logiciels DPA et GFAS utilisés par FHI360 Maintenir à jour le classement des tous les documents comptables et financier du projet Epic du bureau de Lubumbashi. Participer activement à la clôture mensuelle des comptes de FHI 360 RDC Scanner les Vouchers ainsi que toutes les pièces comptables et les télécharger sur le Share point du bureau pays. Prépare les rapprochements des comptes du bilan inclus les réconciliations de la banque et petite caisse du projet Epic Elaborer le Cash projection mensuelle pour approvisionnement des fonds Capturer les avances des partenaires d’implémentation dans les systèmes DPA et GFAS Capturer les réconciliations des avances des partenaires d’implémentation dans les systèmes DPA et GFAS Superviser la gestion du Petty cash de Lubumbashi et ou Mbujimayi NB : la liste des taches ci-haut n’est pas exhaustive et pourra être actualisée dans l’intérêt du bon fonctionnement de FHI360. QUALIFICATIONS REQUISES Diplôme universitaire en comptabilité, management, gestion, finance ou un autre domaine apparent Une expérience professionnelle pertinente d’au moins 3 ans dans le domaine des finances et comptabilité Expérience de travail antérieur avec les programmes financés par le Gouvernement Américain serait un atout considérable Expérience antérieure dans la gestion des partenaires d’exécution du projet est préférée Bonnes capacités de communication et bonne maîtrise écrite et orale de l’anglais et du français ; dautres langues locales (lingala ou swahili) un plus Capable de communiquer de manière claire et positive avec le client et le personnel. Connaissance informatique plus particulièrement en Word et Excel Capacité à respecter les délais avec une forte attention à la cohérence, les détails, et la qualité. Aptitude à voyager dans le pays Présentation du dossier et Dépôt de candidature Tout(e) candidat(e) intéressé(e) est prié(e) de soumettre son Curriculum Vitae (CV) mis à jour avec au moins trois références professionnelles vérifiables (avec mention de leurs fonctions, adresses électroniques et numéros de téléphone), ainsi qu’une lettre de motivation. title: Senior People and Culture Officer
description: Contexte du Projet EpiC RDC Le projet Meeting Targets and Maintaining Epidemic Control (EpiC) RDC, mis en œuvre par FHI 360 et ses partenaires, est financé par le Département dÉtat des États-Unis (DoS), via le Bureau de la Sécurité Sanitaire Mondiale et de la Diplomatie (GHSD). Le projet vise à accélérer la réduction de la mortalité maternelle, néonatale et infantile évitable en fournissant un paquet dinterventions vitales intégrées couvrant la Santé Maternelle et Infantile (SMNE), la Nutrition, et le Paludisme dans neuf provinces de la République Démocratique du Congo (RDC). Le programme adopte une approche géographique par phases, avec quatre provinces désignées à « Pleine Présence » (Haut Katanga, Lualaba, Kasai Oriental, et Lomami) pour un soutien complet et immédiat, et cinq provinces à « Présence Minimale » pour un soutien ciblé sur le paludisme. Alignement avec la Stratégie America First (AF-GHS) Le projet EpiC RDC est strictement guidé par les principes de la nouvelle Stratégie Mondiale de Santé America First (AF-GHS) du gouvernement américain. Cette stratégie marque un tournant majeur dans la coopération internationale en santé et exige du projet : Efficacité et Redressement du Système : La réduction de linefficacité, de la duplication des systèmes de prestation de services et des coûts de lassistance technique non directement liés aux services de première ligne. Appropriation Nationale (Country Ownership) : Le renforcement de lautonomie, de la résilience et de la durabilité du système de santé congolais. Le projet sengage dans un plan de transition quinquennal vers lappropriation complète et le financement par le Gouvernement de la RDC. Intégration : Lobligation de mettre en œuvre un paquet de services totalement intégré (SMNE/Nutrition/Paludisme), en recherchant des synergies avec la Sécurité Sanitaire Mondiale (GHS) et le VIH. RESUME DU POSTE Définit lorientation stratégique en matière de Personnel et de Culture pour le bureau en RDC et assure la supervision ainsi que la gestion des Ressources Humaines pour garantir lefficacité et lefficience opérationnelle du bureau. Développe des processus efficaces en collaborant avec des experts en la matière, des utilisateurs finaux et la direction pour identifier les ruptures de flux de travail dans les domaines fonctionnels, y compris, mais sans sy limiter, ladministration des avantages sociaux, la rémunération, les relations avec les employés, le SIRH (Système d’Information des Ressources Humaines), la paie, et la formation & le développement. Interprète les politiques et les pratiques. Assure la conformité avec les lois et réglementations nationales relatives à la rémunération, aux avantages sociaux, à lemploi, à la paie et, le cas échéant, à lindemnisation des accidents du travail. Gère les problèmes de relations avec les employés, tels que les plaintes de nouveaux employés ou autres préoccupations du personnel. Sert de point de contact pour léquipe et les responsables concernant les questions liées à leurs informations personnelles demployé. Conduit les sessions dintégration des nouvelles recrues ( orientations ) et peut gérer ou faciliter les entretiens de départ des employés ( exit interviews ). Prépare des rapports spéciaux en utilisant les données du SIRH. RESPONSABILITES Sous la supervision de People and Culture Manager, le Senior People and Culture Officer Sert de ressource expérimentée dans plusieurs domaines, y compris, mais sans sy limiter : le SIRH (Système d’Information des Ressources Humaines), la rémunération, les avantages sociaux, le recrutement, la paie, ainsi que la formation et le développement. Sassure que le bureau du projet adhère aux meilleures pratiques en matière de normes demploi, aux politiques de Ressources Humaines et Culture Organisationnelle, ainsi quaux politiques et procédures de FHI 360. Aide à lévaluation des problèmes complexes générés au sein de son bureau, les évalue et recommande des solutions. Promeut de manière proactive des relations client positives et encadre les autres afin dassurer la satisfaction client. Crée et rationalise les rapports et les processus . Mène les processus dintégration ( orientation des nouveaux employés ) et de séparation/entretiens de départ . Prépare et maintient tous les dossiers personnels (physiques et électroniques), en veillant à ce que toutes les informations sur chaque membre du personnel soient à jour, exactes et correctement classées. Mène des analyses des tendances RH et des activités de reporting pour identifier les opportunités et recommander des solutions. Développe et met en œuvre des procédures, y compris des plans de communication, lorientation des nouveaux employés et des programmes de formation . Assure une réponse et une résolution rapides aux questions des employés en surveillant le temps de réponse des autres aux questions reçues. Identifie les questions fréquemment posées et formule des recommandations sur la manière dont linformation devrait être communiquée au personnel. Identifie et résout les problèmes commerciaux globaux grâce à une connaissance approfondie et à jour du droit du travail , du comportement organisationnel, de la gestion du changement, des meilleures pratiques et de la politique de lentreprise. Gère linitiative d amélioration continue et recherche constamment des moyens de rationaliser ou daméliorer lexpérience utilisateur. Peut mener des enquêtes et résoudre les problèmes courants de relations avec les employés . Exécute toute autre tâche/responsabilité connexe qui pourrait lui être assignée. Connaissances et Compétences Appliquées Capacité à gérer les communications orales et écrites de manière autonome . Capable de communiquer efficacement et avec tact avec tous les niveaux de personnel, les individus de cultures diverses et le public. Démontre la capacité dassumer une responsabilité unique et indépendante pour divers projets assignés. Résolution de Problèmes et Impact Travaille sur des tâches typiques dun environnement de bureau administratif RH . Peut travailler sur des projets spéciaux et ad hoc , si nécessaire. A la capacité de reconnaître et de suggérer des améliorations pour les situations qui sécartent des pratiques acceptées ; demande lavis de la direction avant dagir. Les erreurs peuvent avoir un impact sérieux sur les autres membres de léquipe ou le fonctionnement du département et sa capacité à respecter les délais. Supervision Donnée/Reçue Le travail seffectue sous une supervision limitée et des instructions générales sont données pour les nouvelles activités ou les missions spéciales. Propose des idées et des suggestions pour améliorer lefficacité, mais ne modifie pas les procédures sans lapprobation du superviseur. Peut encadrer du personnel junior . FORMATION & QUALIFICATIONS REQUISES * Baccalauréat (Licence) ou son équivalent international. Autres certifications professionnelles/de gestion pertinentes seraient un atout. Expérience Généralement 7 ans et plus dexpérience dans le soutien administratif RH complet dans un environnement de bureau. Compétences avancées dans la suite MS Office (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint et Access) et les systèmes SIRH / Paie. Une expérience de travail antérieure au sein dune organisation non gouvernementale (ONG) est un atout. Exigences Physiques Typiques Environnement de bureau typique. Capacité à passer de longues heures devant un écran dordinateur et à effectuer un travail répétitif au clavier. Capacité à sasseoir et à se tenir debout pendant des périodes prolongées. Capacité à soulever/déplacer jusquà 2,5 kg (conversion approximative de 5 lbs). This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. title: Grants and Finance Officer
description: About FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit human development organization dedicated to improving lives in lasting ways by advancing integrated, locally driven solutions. Our staff includes experts in health, education, nutrition, environment, economic development, civil society, gender, youth, research and technology — creating a unique mix of capabilities to address todays interrelated development challenges. FHI 360 serves more than 70 countries and all U.S. states and territories. We are currently seeking qualified local candidates for the position of: Grants and Finance Officer Description: FHI 360 is seeking a candidate for the position of Grants and Finance Officer for a project to be implemented in Zambezia and Nampula provinces, focused on improving the quality of nutrition, neonatal, maternal, and child health outcomes and immunization, and working with government partners to institutionalize a culture of quality improvement. Under the supervision of the Finance and Operations Manager, the Grants and Finance Officer will support the effective management and oversight of sub-grants under the EpiC Project in Mozambique, implemented in the provinces of Nampula and Zambezia. The position is responsible for supporting the full lifecycle of sub-grants, including pre-award assessments, agreement development, monitoring, financial review, compliance oversight, and close-out. The role will ensure that sub-grantees comply with donor regulations, FHI 360 policies, and applicable financial management standards. The Grants and Finance Officer will work closely with finance, operations, and technical teams to ensure effective coordination of grants administration and financial monitoring. The position will also provide capacity strengthening to partner organizations to improve compliance, financial management , and reporting practices will provide senior-level financial management and oversight for the EpiC Project in Mozambique, supporting project implementation in the provinces of Zambezia and Nampula. The position is based in Quelimane , Zambezia province. Accountab ilities: Grants Management Support the full lifecycle management of sub-grants, including pre-award assessments, agreement preparation, modifications, and close-out processes. Maintain accurate and complete sub-grant files in accordance with FHI 360 and donor requirements. Track sub-grantee compliance with grant terms, conditions, and reporting deadlines. Support the preparation and review of sub-grant agreements and amendments. Ensure grants documentation is properly organized and maintained for audit and compliance purposes . Sub recipient Monitoring and Compliance Conduct routine monitoring of sub-grantees to assess financial, operational, and programmatic compliance. Review subrecipient financial and programmatic reports to ensure accuracy and adherence to donor requirements. Identify potential compliance risks and work with sub-grantees to develop corrective action plans. Support internal and external audits related to sub-grants. Ensure that sub-grantee documentation meets donor and organizational standards. Financial Oversight of Sub-Grants Support the review and verification of partner financial reports and supporting documentation. Coordinate with finance teams to ensure timely processing of partner disbursements. Monitor sub-grantee budget utilization and financial reporting against approved budgets. Assist in tracking partner advances and financial reconciliations. Support financial analysis of partner expenditures and identify discrepancies or financial risks. Partner Capacity Strengthening Provide ongoing technical support to sub-grantees on grants compliance, reporting requirements, and financial management practices. Strengthen partner understanding of donor regulations and FHI 360 grants policies. Coordinate training sessions, mentoring, and follow-up support for partner organizations. Promote best practices in financial management and grant administration among partner organizations. Coordination and Program Support Work closely with finance, operations, and technical teams to align grants management with project implementation. Support the Associate Director, Programs in coordinating and monitoring project activities related to partner implementation. Assist in preparing grants tracking reports and contribute to donor reporting requirements. Support documentation and communication related to sub-grantee performance and compliance. Other Responsibilities Perform other duties as assigned by the supervisor or project leadership to support the objectives of the EpiC Project. Qualifications and Experience Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Business Administration, International Development, or a related field. Master’s degree is an advantage. Minimum 3–5 years of experience in grants management within donor-funded projects. Demonstrated experience managing sub-grants for multiple partner organizations. Strong knowledge of donor compliance requirements, preferably U.S. Government (USG) or USAID-funded programs. Experience supporting capacity building of local partner organizations. Experience working with financial management and grants administration systems. Skills and Competencies Strong organizational and attention-to-detail skills. Ability to interpret and apply donor regulations and grant agreements. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and relationship management skills. Ability to work independently and manage multiple grants simultaneously. High level of integrity, ethics, and confidentiality. Ability to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines. Technical Skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office , particularly Excel and Word. Experience with grants management systems and financial reporting tools. Ability to quickly learn and adapt to organizational systems and donor reporting platforms. Languages Fluency in English is required. Portuguese proficiency is strongly preferred. Travel Requirements: Less than 10% ** Or an equivalent combination of relevant education and/or experience sufficient to successfully p erform the key responsibilities of the job. title: Senior Finance Officer - Quelimane
description: About FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit human development organization dedicated to improving lives in lasting ways by advancing integrated, locally driven solutions. Our staff includes experts in health, education, nutrition, environment, economic development, civil society, gender, youth, research and technology — creating a unique mix of capabilities to address todays interrelated development challenges. FHI 360 serves more than 70 countries and all U.S. states and territories. We are currently seeking qualified local candidates for the position of: Senior Finance Officer Description: FHI 360 is seeking a candidate for the position of Senior Finance Officer (SFO) for a project to be implemented in Zambezia and Nampula provinces, focused on improving the quality of nutrition, neonatal, maternal, and child health outcomes and immunization, and working with government partners to institutionalize a culture of quality improvement. Under the supervision of the Finance and Ope rations Manager, the SFO will provide senior-level financial management and oversight for the EpiC Project in Mozambique, supporting project implementation in the provinces of Zambezia and Nampula. The position is responsible for coordinating financial and accounting operations, ensuring accurate financial monitoring, and supporting finance and operations teams to maintain the fiscal integrity of the project. The role includes financial analysis, preparation and review of project financial reports, oversight of financial compliance, and support to project leadership in monitoring financial performance trends. The SFO also contributes to strengthening internal controls, managing banking operations, and overseeing financial management of subawards and partner activities. The position ensures compliance with donor requirements, FHI 360 policies and procedures, and applicable Mozambican laws and regulations . The position is based in Quelimane , Zambezia province. Accountab ilities: Financial Management and Analysis Coordinate financial and accounting operations to ensure effective financial management of the project. Monitor project expenditures and ensure alignment with approved budgets and financial plans. Analyze financial performance trends and provide recommendations to project leadership. Prepare and review monthly financial reports and expenditure analysis reports. Support preparation and monitoring of financial forecasts and budget execution. Ensure the project is adequately funded for uninterrupted implementation of activities and that financial resources are available when needed. Accounting Oversight Oversee the accuracy and completeness of accounting transactions recorded in the financial system (GFAS). Review financial transactions and supporting documentation to ensure compliance with FHI 360 policies and donor regulations. S upport monthly financial close processes and ensure timely submission of financial reports. Review reconciliations of bank accounts, advances, and financial balances. Banking and Treasury Management Oversee project banking operations, including transactions, currency conversions, and banking documentation. Manage bank account administration, including signatories and banking compliance requirements. Monitor project bank account balances and ensure proper cash flow management. Coordinate communication with banking institutions regarding operational matters. Subaward and Partner Financial Management Review financial reports submitted by partners and ensure compliance with subaward agreements and donor requirements. Support the review of partner budgets, amendments, and obligations worksheets. Monitor partner advances and reconcile advances in the GFAS system. Prepare partner disbursements and ensure accurate documentation of financial transactions. Monitor VAT related to community-based organizations (CBOs) and prepare consolidated VAT reports for the project. Strengthen financial oversight of subawards and identify risks or compliance concerns. Compliance and Internal Controls Ensure full compliance with FHI 360 financial policies and procedures. Ensure adherence to donor regulations, particularly USAID financial management requirements. Ensure compliance with Mozambican fiscal and financial regulations. Strengthen internal financial control systems and financial risk management processes. Support internal and external audits and ensure proper documentation and follow-up on audit findings. Financial Reporting and Systems Prepare and submit the SFR tracker and other required donor financial reporting tools. Ensure that all SFRs and amendment agreements are properly recorded and maintained in the system. Maintain financial monitoring trackers and ensure timely financial reporting. Ensure partner financial monitoring tools and documentation are updated regularly. Capacity Building and Team Support Provide financial guidance and mentorship to Finance Officers and other finance staff. Support financial training for FHI 360 partners, technical teams, and program staff to strengthen financial compliance and monitoring. Promote best practices in financial management across the project team. Other Responsibilities Perform other finance and operational tasks assigned by the supervisor. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field. Minimum of 8 years of progressive responsible accounting and financial management in international development programs or donor-funded projects. Demonstrated experience applying USAID financial management rules and regulations. Proven experience ensuring compliance with Mozambican fiscal and financial regulations. Experience in subaward or grant financial management , preferably within large donor-funded programs. Demonstrated leadership experience in supervising or mentoring finance staff. Experience working with financial management systems and internal control frameworks. Proven experience in transferring knowledge into operational plan with clients collaboration and sustainable buy - in . Substantial experience using computerized financial information systems. Articulate, professional and able to communicate in a clear, positive manner with clients and staff. Skills and Competencies Strong analytical and financial reporting skills. High level of attention to detail and accuracy. Ability to analyze financial data and provide strategic recommendations. Strong leadership and mentoring skills. Excellent organizational and time management skills. Ability to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines. High level of integrity, ethics, and confidentiality. Technical Skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Experience with financial systems and accounting platforms (GFAS or similar) Ability to quickly learn corporate systems and financial reporting tools. Languages Fluency in Portuguese is required . English proficiency is an advantage . Travel Requirements : Less than 10% ** Or an equivalent combination of relevant education and/or experience sufficient to successfully perform the key responsibilities of the job. title: Logistics and Supply Chain Specialist
description: ABOUT FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit organization that mobilizes research, resources and relationships so that people everywhere can access the opportunities they need to lead full, healthy lives. Our staff of more than 2,200 experts work in over 50 countries around the world. PROJECT OVERVIEW : FHI 360 has worked in the Philippines for over 30 years and have taken a comprehensive approach by providing holistic and cutting-edge solutions to help address the country’s health and development challenges. Leveraging a blend of global expertise and local insights, we have consistently strengthened community capacities, enhanced systems, and reinforced institutional frameworks for a sustainable impact. In partnership with the Government of the Philippines, civil society, academia, and the private sector, FHI 360 delivers integrated programs across critical sectors. Our initiatives have spanned essential health services – including HIV and epidemic control (COVID-19 and TB), health system strengthening, and global health security. The U.S. Government’s (USG) Global Health Security (GHS) program provides life-saving assistance to prevent, detect, and respond to infectious disease threats which pose risks for public health outbreaks that could cost lives. As part of the USG’s GHS program, the STRengthening Infectious disease DEtection Systems (STRIDES) Activity advances cross-sectoral efforts to successfully identify, manage, and respond to infectious disease threats; detect and prevent further outbreaks; and instill confidence and trust in a country’s disease detection and surveillance structures and services. POSITION SUMMARY The Logistics and Supply Chain Specialist leads and oversees logistics, supply chain, and inventory management for the STRIDES Activity in the Philippines, reporting to the Project Operations Manager. This role ensures timely availability of essential commodities through effective and efficient communication and collaboration with various members of the project team. The position strengthens supply chain systems to support STRIDES objectives in infectious disease detection, surveillance, and emergency response. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Oversee supply chain operations including forecasting, procurement, inventory management, warehousing, and distribution of office and health commodities. Develop and implement contingency and surge logistics plans for emergency response and outbreak scenarios. Monitor inventory levels, consumption, and stock replenishment to prevent shortages and wastage. Provide technical guidance to strengthen national and subnational supply chain capacity through tools, SOPs, and training. Coordinate with government partners, facilities, and technical teams for seamless commodity flow. Analyze supply chain data and prepare reports, dashboards, and briefings for project leadership. Ensure compliance with client, donor, national, and organizational requirements and regulations. Support emergency procurement and rapid deployment during public health crises. Collaborate with procurement specialists and operational teams for integrated execution. Ensures adequacy of office supplies at all times. Facilitates canvassing and purchasing of supplies/services Acts as asset custodian. Responsible for general maintenance and repair of office equipment (i.e., computers, printers, fax machine, air conditioning, etc.), office fixtures, and other project assets; Establishes and maintains a records management system for logistics documents Execute additional logistics/supply chain responsibilities to support project objectives as assigned by the Project Operations Manager or the Project Director. EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE Bachelors degree in supply chain management, logistics, public health, or related field (Masters preferred). Minimum 5 years of experience in supply chain/logistics management, preferably in public health or NGO settings. REQUIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS Strong expertise in health commodity supply chain management and emergency logistics. Proven ability to manage inventory, forecasting, and distribution in fast-paced environments. Excellent analytical skills for data monitoring, gap analysis, and performance reporting. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel for inventory tracking and reporting. Superior coordination and relationship-building with government, partners, and technical teams. Strong problem-solving, organizational, and communication skills (English and Tagalog). Experience with internationally funded programs preferred. Adaptable team player comfortable working across time zones. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. description: ABOUT FHI 360: FHI 360 is a nonprofit organization that mobilizes research, resources and relationships so that people everywhere can access the opportunities they need to lead full, healthy lives. Our staff of more than 2,200 experts work in over 50 countries around the world. Leveraging a blend of global expertise and local insights, we have consistently strengthened community capacities, enhanced systems, and reinforced institutional frameworks for a sustainable impact. In partnership with the Government of the Philippines, civil society, academia, and the private sector, FHI 360 delivers integrated programs across critical sectors. The U.S. Government’s (USG) Global Health Security (GHS) program provides life-saving assistance to prevent, detect, and respond to infectious disease threats which pose risks for public health outbreaks that could cost lives. As part of the USG’s GHS program, the STRengthening Infectious disease DEtection Systems (STRIDES) Activity advances cross-sectoral efforts to successfully identify, manage, and respond to infectious disease threats; detect and prevent further outbreaks; and instill confidence and trust in a country’s disease detection and surveillance structures and services. POSITION SUMMARY The Project Officer supports the Project Operations Manager in executing operational aspects of the STRIDES Activity in the Philippines. This role provides skilled coordination of day-to-day activities across all project objectives, serves as a liaison for partners, and ensures strong integration with technical and finance teams. This role also provides programmatic and technical support to implementation teams in the conduct of their activities. With comprehensive operational expertise, the position drives seamless implementation, advancing STRIDES mission in infectious disease detection, surveillance, and response. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Coordinate daily operations across STRIDES objectives, including assessments, trainings, workshops, and simulation exercises. Act as point of contact for partners and other stakeholders, managing communications, planning, and issue resolution. Collaborate with technical and financial teams to align activities and budget with work plans, deliverables, and project objectives. Facilitate government approvals and stakeholder engagements for program initiatives and events. Develop operational documentation such as agendas, reports, briefings, ToR, SOW, and progress updates. Establishes and maintains a records management system for program documents Support the monitoring of project milestones, deliverables, and risks, providing clear insights and recommendations. Support event planning and execution, partnering with logistics/procurement specialists and implementation teams for operational efficiency. Build and sustain effective relationships with technical staff, government counterparts, and partners. Support MEL team in maintaining a comprehensive tracking systems (logs, matrices) for activities, performance metrics, and compliance. Handle additional operational duties as assigned by the Project Operations Manager or Project Director EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE Bachelor’s degree in business administration, public health, international development, or related field (Master’s preferred). Minimum 3 years of experience in project operations or coordination, preferably in NGOs, public health, or international development. REQUIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS Strong operational coordination skills, with expertise in managing multi-stakeholder environments and project execution. Proven partner and relationship management across technical teams, government, NGOs, and subcontractors. Excellent organizational abilities for tracking progress, risk management, and ensuring timely delivery. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, Outlook) and virtual collaboration tools. Superior interpersonal, communication (oral/written), problem-solving, and facilitation skills. Fluency in English and Tagalog, with familiarity of Philippines health systems an advantage. Proactive, adaptable team player committed to client and organizational compliance and project success. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. title: People & Culture Associate - Morocco
description: Job Summary The People & Culture Associate provides operational support for core P&C functions for the US Government-funded STRengthening Infectious Disease DEtection Systems (STRIDES) project in Morocco. This includes recruitment coordination, onboarding, benefits administration, employee data management, and policy compliance monitoring. The role involves independent work and requires initiative to address standard employee needs while ensuring alignment with FHI 360 policies and Moroccan labor laws. **This position will work out of the STRIDES office in Rabat, Morocco. Accountabilities Coordinates end-to-end recruitment support, including vacancy postings, candidate screening, interview scheduling, and reference checks. Maintains and updates HRIS data, ensuring accuracy and completeness. Supports country performance management processes, including goal-setting and reviews. Supports payroll and benefits processing and liaises with vendors as needed. Supports employee onboarding and offboarding processes. Provides first-line response to employee questions regarding P&C policies and benefits. Assists with compliance reporting and documentation for audits. Performs other related duties/ responsibilities as may be assigned. Applied Knowledge & Skills Working knowledge of P&C procedures and employment regulations. Strong attention to detail and data accuracy. Good communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to handle confidential information appropriately. Problem Solving & Impact Resolves routine P&C issues independently, escalating complex matters. Contributes to the efficiency and accuracy of P&C services. Supervision Given/Received Could report to local P&C Officer, Senior P&C Officer, P&C Manager, Senior P&C Manager, or Regional Level People & Culture Partner (depending on size and complexity of country office portfolio). Works independently on routine tasks with occasional supervision. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree in human resources, business administration, or related field preferred. 3 years of P&C experience, preferably in an INGO or similar environment. Experience supporting US Government-funded contracts and an understanding of applicable rules and regulations strongly preferred. Written and spoken professional proficiency in English required. Travel Requirements Occasional travel within the country. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. title: M&E Officer
description: Titre du Poste : M&E Officer Lieu du poste : diverse locations Type de poste : CDD Contexte Family Health International (FHI 360) est une Organisation Non Gouvernementale (ONG) à but non lucratif, dédiée à l’amélioration de la vie de façon durable via des solutions intégrées. FHI 360, est présente dans plus de 60 pays au monde et dans tous les États et territoires des États-Unis et intervient dans les domaines de la santé, de l’éducation, de la nutrition, de lenvironnement, de léconomie et du développement, de la recherche et technologie, créant ainsi une combinaison unique de capacités pour relever les défis du développement. Au Sénégal, FHI 360 mets en œuvre différents projets visant à améliorer la qualité de vie de population et soutenir le développement humain. En appui à la stratégie nationale de lutte contre les maladies, les différents projets fournissent une assistance technique stratégique et des prestations directes de services pour améliorer la prévention, le dépistage et le traitement des populations cibles, en particulier les groupes les plus vulnérables et populations prioritaires. FHI 360 Sénégal recrute pour neuf (13) Chargé(e)s de Suivi et Evaluation pour différentes régions (Dakar, Louga, Matam, Diourbel, Kaolack, Kolda, Sédhiou, Tambacounda et Kédougou). Principales Activités Le/La Chargé(e) de Suivi et Evaluation Officer sera membre de l’équipe d’Information Stratégique du Bureau FHI360 Senegal et travailleront en étroite collaboration avec le Conseiller Senior en suivi et évaluation de FHI360 Sénégal. Sous sa supervision directe, il/elle appuiera la mise en place des activités du programme, et contribuera efficacement à la mise en œuvre du système de collecte, compilation, analyse et de transmission des données. Il/elle assurera la disponibilité des rapports des projets ainsi que le renforcement des capacités des partenaires de mise en œuvre en matière de suivi et évaluation. Responsabilités et Tâches Spécifiques : De façon spécifique le Chargé(e) de Suivi et Evaluation assurera les principales responsabilités et tâches suivantes : Contribuer à la mise en œuvre et à la pérennisation d’un système de suivi-évaluation qui permette de s’assurer que les partenaires de mise en œuvre (PMO) et cliniques soumettent dans les délais, des rapports de suivi réguliers et de bonne qualité ; Procéder à une vérification directe des données pour assurer la cohérence interne et la validité des données de suivi des activités du programme fournies par tous les PMO et cliniques, à travers des contrôles périodiques des données et des visites d’audit des données auprès des PMO et cliniques ; Soutenir la mise en œuvre de bonnes pratiques de gestion des données au sein des PMO et cliniques afin de garantir une mesure adéquate des progrès accomplis en matière de performances ; Assurer le remplissage correct et systématique par les PMO et cliniques, des outils nationaux pour le suivi de l’offre de service de sante aux bénéficiaires ; Assurer la saisie des données par les PMO dans les bases de données nationales (DHIS2 E-Tracker), des projets a FHI360 et des bailleurs de fonds ; Soutenir la saisie des données des projets a FHI360 sur une base trimestrielle ; Contribuer à la préparation et à une soumission de qualité en temps opportun de tous les rapports et documents de projet requis suivant les périodicités convenues avec le bailleur et les programmes ; Faciliter l’interprétation et l’exploitation des données de S&E et promouvoir l’utilisation appropriée des données du programme afin d’éclairer la prise de décisions ; Analyser les données des cliniques et PMO des projets et conduire des réunions de revue de performances avec élaboration conjointe de plans d’amélioration avec lesdits PMO et cliniques ; Contribuer aux activités de renforcement de capacités dans les domaines du suivi, de l’évaluation et de la production de rapports, à l’intention des PMO et cliniques ; Exécuter toute autre tâche demandée par le Conseiller Senior Régional en Information Stratégique de l’Afrique de l’Ouest de FHI360 et le superviseur direct afin d’appuyer la gestion, la supervision, le suivi et l’évaluation de la mise en œuvre du projet EpiC Qualifications et Compétences Requises : Être titulaire d’un BAC 4 en Santé Publique, – Démographie, Sociologie, Épidémiologie, Biostatistique, Psychologie ou dans tout autre domaine connexe ; Avoir au minimum de 2 ans d’expérience dans le domaine du suivi, de l’évaluation et de la recherche liés aux programmes de santé publique, y compris dans la mise en œuvre de programmes de lutte contre le VIH ; Avoir une expérience avérée en matière de détermination d’objectifs de lutte contre le VIH/sida et de production de rapports y relatifs ; Avoir d’excellentes aptitudes rédactionnelles et verbales, en relations interpersonnelles et en présentation ; Avoir une bonne expérience dans la synthèse d’information et production de rapports y relatifs ; Avoir une expérience avérée dans l’utilisation de E-tracker pour le suivi individualisé des clients ; Avoir une expérience prouvée dans le développement des indicateurs d’un projet dans le domaine de la santé ; Avoir au moins une bonne expérience des logiciels Microsoft (Excel, Word) et des logiciels de S&E / Statistique ; Avoir une expérience de l’utilisation d’un DHIS2 et de son module E-tracker ; Avoir une expérience avérée et une bonne connaissance de l’analyse des données, et l’élaboration de systèmes de S&E dans les pays en développement ; Avoir des compétences linguistiques avérées en français et minimales en anglais. Conditions de Voyage : 30-40% voyages domestiques. This job posting summarizes the main duties of the job. title: Procurement Officer
description: About FHI 360 FHI 360 is a nonprofit human development organization dedicated to improving lives in lasting ways by advancing integrated, locally driven solutions. Our staff includes experts in health, education, nutrition, environment, economic development, civil society, gender, youth, research, and technology — creating a unique mix of capabilities to address todays interrelated development challenges. FHI 360 serves more than 70 countries and all U.S. states and territories. We are currently seeking qualified candidates for the position of Procurement Officer. Description FHI 360 is seeking a qualified candidate for the position of Procurement Officer for EpiC MNCH, a project to be implemented in the provinces of Nampula and Zambezia , focused on improving the quality of maternal, neonatal, and child health outcomes and working with government partners to institutionalize a culture of quality improvement. The Procurement Officer will be responsible for purchasing goods and services and ensuring that the procurement process is efficiently managed from requisition to final delivery, including maintaining procurement trackers, monitoring orders, and ensuring timely delivery of goods and services . S/he must be able to formulate, analyze, and interpret contractual documents, tenders, and Requests for Proposals (RFPs) to determine their suitability, integrity, and compliance with applicable laws, donor regulations, and organizational policies. The selected candidate will be based in Zambezia and will report to t he E nterpri se Service Finance Manager . Accountabilities Lead the procurement process, including reviewing and processing purchase requisitions , following up on r equests and ensuring t imely delivery. Participate in supplier selection processes, including requesting quotations and negotiating competitive pricing and terms. Prepare formal procurement documentation (bid comparative analysis, memoranda, purchase requisitions) through purchase order issuance; follow up on orders and prepare procurement status reports. Update procurement trackers and records and ensure proper filing and archiving of procurement documents in compliance with audit requirements. Conduct negotiations of contracts and agreements that protect the organization’s interests and provide best value to the project. Engage proactively with project staff to establish and maintain positive and effective working relationships with suppliers and service providers. Maintain appropriate documentation to ensure that procurement processes, decisions, and contractual agreements are accurately recorded for accountability and audit purposes (evaluation reports, supplier selection minutes, memoranda, and other supporting documents). Prepare routine contracts for the supply of goods and services in line with organizational and donor regulations. Ensure that procurement transactions are carried out in a timely and compliant manner. Submit invoices with complete supporting documentation to the Finance Unit for payment processing. Prepare and submit monthly procurement activity reports. Review and evaluate supplier performance; track deliveries and coordinate with suppliers regarding contractual obligations. Liaise with requesters and provide regular updates on procurement status. Perform other related duties as assigned in support of the procurement unit. Required Qualifications Bachelors degree in business administration , Supply Chain Management, Procurement, or a related field; a professional certification in procurement (e.g., CIPS) is an added advantage. Knowledge of U.S. Government/DOS rules and regulations and experience applying donor procurement requirements. 3–5 years of relevant experience in procurement with an INGO or international organization. Demonstrated knowledge of procurement standards, competitive bidding processes, and contract management. Strong analytical, negotiation, and relationship management skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook). Ability to work with minimal supervision and manage high-volume workflows under tight deadlines. Typical Physical Demands Typical office environment. Ability to spend extended periods working on a computer and performing repetitive keyboard tasks. Ability to sit and stand for extended periods. Ability to lift/move up to 5 lbs. Technology to be Used Personal computer/laptop; Microsoft Office 365 applications (including SharePoint and Teams); mobile phone technology; and standard office equipment. Travel Requirements Limited travel within Zambezia Province may be required , as needed. This job description summarizes the main duties of the position.
